title: cynoglossum cheirifolium subsp. heterocarpum
intro: Cynoglossum cheirifolium subsp. heterocarpum, also known as the Heterocarpous Hound's Tongue, is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia and the Himalayas. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as an infusion, tincture, or poultice, and has also been used in folk remedies to treat various ailments.
